title: Email
description: Email is a field-specialized wrapper around the shared input foundation with native `type="email"`, built-in clear affordance, and address-oriented defaults like email autocomplete. Use it for invites, contact points, billing addresses, and notification settings.
intro: Use `ui-email` when the value is specifically an email address and should benefit from native browser validation, mobile keyboard hints, and shared field behavior. It inherits the design-system field API for labels, help text, size, variant, readonly, disabled, and validation messaging.

featureSections.title: Basic email field
featureSections.description: Good default for a single contact point. Keep the label explicit and use help text only when the user needs extra context about where the address will be used.

featureSections.title: Label position and size
featureSections.description: Label placement should match surrounding form rhythm, while size helps the same field fit compact tables, dialogs, or roomier settings surfaces.

featureSections.title: Validation states
featureSections.description: Pair native email semantics with reactive-form validation so required and malformed addresses surface clearly before users submit an invite or workflow step.

featureSections.title: Readonly, disabled, and autocomplete
featureSections.description: Readonly is useful for visible identifiers that should still be copyable, disabled preserves context without editing, and autocomplete keeps the field aligned with browser email behavior.

featureSections.title: Multiple address form
featureSections.description: A practical pattern for settings screens: primary contact, billing, and alert routing in one consistent field family instead of mixing generic text inputs.

featureSections.title: Invite panel composition
featureSections.description: A stronger end-to-end example: contextual copy, a single email field, and direct actions for invite or link sharing. This is usually how email fields appear in product flows.

accessibility.title: Label and name
accessibility.body: Accessible naming follows the shared field model: prefer a visible `label`, or provide `ariaLabel` when no visible label is appropriate. Help and error text contribute through `aria-describedby`.

accessibility.title: Native email semantics
accessibility.body: The inner input uses native `type="email"`, so browsers can offer email-specific keyboards, autofill behavior, and baseline validity checks. `autocomplete` defaults to `email` when you do not override it.

accessibility.title: Keyboard and actions
accessibility.body:
This is a standard text-entry field, so focus and editing use native input behavior. When the field is editable and non-empty, the built-in clear action becomes available beside the mail affordance.

| Aspect | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| focus | native input receives focus |
| typing | native email input behavior |
| clear action | appears only when editable and value is present |
| described by | help and error text IDs when present |
| autocomplete | defaults to `email` |

apiSections.title: EmailComponent - ui-email
apiSections.lead: Standalone email-specific field built on the shared field foundation. It keeps the same layout and validation hooks as other field components while enforcing native email input semantics.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

paragraphs: The component extends the shared `FieldComponent`, so value access, validation hooks, keyboard handling, and focus state follow the same base behavior as the rest of the field family.
On focus, a populated editable email input selects its full value to make replacement faster in address-editing flows.
blocks.title: Notes
